chunk_text: Iron deficiency anemia can cause fatigue, weakness, dizziness, headaches, pale skin, and shortness of breath. Symptoms may be mild at first but can become more
first but can become more noticeable over time. Clinical evaluation often considers symptom severity, possible blood loss, and diet history.
Low dietary iron intake and poor absorption can contribute to iron deficiency. A clinician may look for patterns in fatigue, weakness, concentration issues, and other
weakness, concentration issues, and other related symptoms while considering nutrition and underlying causes.
